rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The data-writer container in Deployment data-service is continuously inserting 500 large uncompressed PostgreSQL TOAST rows every 30 seconds with no data retention or cleanup, causing PVC data-service-data (512Mi, StorageClass lvms-vg1/TopoLVM) to fill at ~211 KB/s and projected to exhaust capacity within 1 hour. predict_linear projects ~1.396 GB used in 1 hour — nearly 3× the provisioned 512Mi capacity.
causalChain: Signal: PVRunwayShort alert — PVC data-service-data in demo-pvc-forecast projected to exhaust 512Mi capacity within 1 hour
Why? kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes growing at ~211 KB/s; predict_linear projects 1.396 GB used in 1 hour vs 512Mi provisioned
Why? PostgreSQL data directory on the PVC is growing rapidly due to continuous large INSERT operations
Why? The data-writer container runs an unbounded loop inserting 500 large uncompressed TOAST rows every 30 seconds with no DELETE or cleanup

Root cause: Deployment data-service contains a data-writer container configured to write large uncompressed payloads (STORAGE EXTERNAL, md5 aggregates) in a tight infinite loop with no data retention policy, against a 512Mi PVC that is too small for the write rate

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting Deployment/data-service because its data-writer container spec defines the write behavior (loop frequency, payload size, STORAGE EXTERNAL) that causes the PVC to fill. The PVC itself is the symptom reporter. Modifying the Deployment — either by adding a cleanup sidecar, reducing insert frequency, enabling compression, or increasing PVC size via PVC resize — directly resolves the issue.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single Deployment with one PVC affected. The data-writer container is the sole writer. Remediation is well-scoped: modify the Deployment's data-writer container behavior or resize the PVC. No other workloads are affected.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: PVC spec and status, Deployment spec (both containers), pod status and logs, Prometheus volume metrics (used, capacity, rate, predict_linear), PVC events, resource context/remediation history. Did not investigate StorageClass capacity limits on the node (TopoLVM LVM group free space) — relevant if PVC expansion is the chosen remediation.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced from alert signal → Prometheus metrics confirming growth rate → pod/container inspection revealing data-writer loop → Deployment spec showing no cleanup logic. The data-writer script was fully inspected and confirmed as the direct cause. Cannot go deeper without application source code intent (this appears intentional by design as a stress test workload).
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by tool output: PVC spec from kubectl (512Mi, lvms-vg1, Bound); Prometheus kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes (88,911,872 bytes used, 469,762,048 bytes capacity); rate metric (211,547 bytes/s); predict_linear result (1,396,441,193 bytes in 1h); pod spec showing data-writer shell script with STORAGE EXTERNAL and 500-row batches every 30s; container logs confirming active inserts. No inaccessible data sources.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for Deployment data-service (confirmed via get_namespaced_resource_context — tier1 and tier2 history both empty).
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Node disk pressure — ruled out: PVC is on TopoLVM (lvms-vg1), node-level disk was not flagged, and the metric is PVC-specific. (2) PostgreSQL bloat from VACUUM not running — possible contributor but secondary; the primary driver is net new inserts with no deletes. (3) Pod restart causing data accumulation from previous runs — range data shows a reset to ~56 MiB suggesting a restart occurred, but growth resumed immediately, confirming the loop restarts and continues filling.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.95: Starting at 1.0, -0.03 for not verifying available LVM group free space on the node (relevant if PVC expansion is chosen), -0.02 for the metric rate warning that kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes may not be a true counter (though the gauge behavior observed is consistent and the growth rate is confirmed by range data).

contributingFactors: data-writer container uses STORAGE EXTERNAL on the payload column, bypassing PostgreSQL TOAST compression and maximizing raw disk usage per row
500 rows of large md5-aggregate payloads inserted every 30 seconds — unbounded write loop with no DELETE or VACUUM logic
PVC provisioned at only 512Mi via lvms-vg1 (TopoLVM) — undersized for the write rate being generated
predict_linear over 5 minutes projects 1.396 GB used in 1 hour — nearly 3× the 512Mi capacity
No data retention, rotation, or TTL policy exists in the data-writer workload

kaResponse.analysis: The data-writer container in Deployment data-service is continuously inserting 500 large uncompressed PostgreSQL TOAST rows every 30 seconds with no data retention or cleanup, causing PVC data-service-data (512Mi, StorageClass lvms-vg1/TopoLVM) to fill at ~211 KB/s and projected to exhaust capacity within 1 hour. predict_linear projects ~1.396 GB used in 1 hour — nearly 3× the provisioned 512Mi capacity.
